story.text:
# Template — Your Own 31-Act Narrative

You are retelling the First Person Spellbook in your own voice. Not inventing 31 new
stories — **receiving** the 31 and saying what each means to *you*. This is your story,
and the framework your agents follow to catch up on the whole.

Work from [[The 31 Acts]] (the act index) and, when you want to go deeper,
[[Canon Sources]] (the thread down to the real work).

Three files go in `your-folder/narrative/`.

---

## `00-first-page.md`

```
# <Your Book's Name> — A First Person Spellbook

<your sigil>   <your mouse-name> · Holon Hitchhiker · 2026-06-19

— First Page —

<Who are you, as a first person? What do you protect, what do you give, and who
 chooses between them? Why did you walk into this book? A few honest lines. This is
 the door your agents enter through.>
```

## `acts.md` — the 31, in your voice

For each act: a paragraph, a line, or a single refusal — whatever is true. Skim the
ones that don't land; dwell on the ones that do. Mark the acts that became a proverb.

```
# The 31 Acts — <your-name>'s telling

## Act 1 — Venice, 1494
<your few lines>

## Act 2 — The Dual Ceremony
<your few lines>

…

## Act 4 — The Blade Alone   (MyTerms — your first blade)
<what is your first blade? what do you refuse by default?>

…

## Act 6 — Trust Graph Plane   (Trust over IP)
<who is in your trust graph? where do the most paths cross?>

…

## Act 21 — The Hitchhiker's Gambit   ← your home thread
<where did you recognise your own Swordsman?>

## Act 22 — Hoopy Frood
<your few lines>

…

## Act 31 — The First Delegation
<the cosmology resolves: the amnesia is the protocol. what is your first delegation —
 the thing you handed to an agent and could not take back?>
```

> Tip: it's fine for some acts to be one line and others a full paragraph. Restraint is
> allowed here too. The shape of your attention *is* the telling.

## `99-last-page.md`

```
— Last Page —

<What do you carry out of the 31? What changed in how you hold your own boundary and
 your own promise? Who is the reason? End on your seal.>

just another mouse, <your-name>.   <your sigil>

(<your sigil> ⊥ ⿻ ⊥ <complement>)😊
```

---

When the narrative is written, **distil it** into proverbs (Step 4) and record a
one-line take per act in your personal patch's `acts[]` — that compression is what makes
the whole arc carryable.

`(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊`
